description: body.origin_registry MUST NOT be a DID URI, even though did:web:registry.example.com denotes the same registry. The field's schema type is a DNS hostname (acdp-common.schema.json#/$defs/hostname) — the colon characters make a DID value fail the pattern. The DID form belongs only in capabilities.registry_did (RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.1). Storing it in origin_registry is the implementation error called out in RFC-ACDP-0002 §3.1.

checks: a registry MUST NOT assign this value to origin_registry
a consumer validating a retrieved body MUST reject it as schema_violation

rationale: Pins the conformance violation: a DID URI in origin_registry. A registry that stores the did:web form here (instead of the bare hostname) is non-conformant, and a consumer validating the retrieved body against acdp-context-body.schema.json MUST reject it. Companion positive case: body-001.
